pattern: Every subarray's cost only ever grows as you extend it in either direction, so sliding a left boundary forward while tracking the running max and min with monotonic deques turns an all-pairs cost check into a single linear sweep.
hints: Brute-force checking every one of the O(n^2) subarrays and recomputing max/min for each one is too slow once the array can hold up to 100000 elements -- look for a way to reuse work as the window slides instead of restarting from scratch.

For a fixed right end, growing the window by moving the left boundary further left can only ever keep the (max - min) gap the same or make it bigger, and it only ever adds to the length too -- so once a window's cost exceeds k, no larger window sharing that same right end can ever recover, which means a two-pointer left boundary only ever needs to move forward.

Maintain the current window's running maximum and minimum with two monotonic deques (one kept decreasing, one kept increasing) as the right pointer advances one step at a time; shrink from the left (popping from the deques whenever their front index falls out of the window) while the cost exceeds k, then add (right - left + 1) to the running count for every right endpoint.

edge_inputs.description: small mixed array with one subarray excluded
single element, cost is always zero
all values equal, every subarray has cost zero
repeated blocks of equal values separated by a jump, k=0 only counts constant-value runs
strictly increasing sequence, tests the two-pointer moving forward across several steps
strictly decreasing sequence, mirrors the increasing case
two extreme constraint values, k=0 excludes the pair entirely
single element at the maximum allowed value
k at its maximum with a tiny array so every subarray is included

stress.description: fully random values across the whole allowed range with a moderate k threshold, exercising a real mix of included and excluded windows
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, 10**12)
    return [nums, k]

stress.description: clustered runs of a small value pool with occasional jumps and a small k, stressing the deque bookkeeping under many ties
stress.note: frequent equal-value runs exercise deque tie-handling (nums[max_dq[-1]] <= nums[r] / >= nums[r] popping)
